source_note: Starter 3-year (2022–2024) means of monthly Nord Pool day-ahead prices per SE1–SE4, converted to öre/kWh. Note the large north/south asymmetry during the 2022 energy crisis: SE1/SE2 stayed relatively low (~45–55 öre) thanks to northern hydro surplus, while SE3/SE4 spiked (~90–115 öre) from continental gas-linked prices — the 3-yr mean inherits that gap. These are starter estimates; a production build should re-derive from Nord Pool or Ei's Elprisstatistik archive and ship a data-freshness check (warn when generated_at > 365 days old).
zone_boundary_simplification: Zone assignment from latitude is a rough north-to-south slice: SE1 lat ≥ 65, SE2 61 ≤ lat < 65, SE3 57 ≤ lat < 61, SE4 lat < 57. Real Svenska kraftnät boundaries follow grid topology, not parallels, so coastal towns near a boundary may be misclassified.

SE1.name: Luleå (Norra Sverige)
SE1.source: Nord Pool SE1 monthly day-ahead 2022–2024 mean

SE2.name: Sundsvall (Norra Mellansverige)
SE2.source: Nord Pool SE2 monthly day-ahead 2022–2024 mean

SE3.name: Stockholm (Södra Mellansverige)
SE3.source: Nord Pool SE3 monthly day-ahead 2022–2024 mean

SE4.name: Malmö (Södra Sverige)
SE4.source: Nord Pool SE4 monthly day-ahead 2022–2024 mean
